name: Syrian Arabic (Damascene)
notes: Syrian Arabic — Damascene (شامي) urban koine, the most influential prestige form of Levantine. INPUT CONTRACT: fully-diacritized Arabic (short vowels surface only when written). ق qaf → /ʔ/ in the Damascus koine (rural/Bedouin /ɡ/ retained via the Levantine parent's variant list). ج jim → /ʒ/. Interdentals merged to stops in urban speech (θ→/t/, ð→/d/, ظ/ض→/dˤ/), conserved by rural/Druze speakers. Diphthongs /aj aw/ → [eː oː] and emphasis backing /a aː/→[ɑ ɑː] are inherited from the Levantine parent's allophone_rules. Vowel system: five long /iː eː aː oː uː/ over three contrastive short /i a u/; [e o ə] are allophones of /i u/ (Almbark & Hellmuth 2015, an acoustic study of fifteen Damascene speakers, §3-4). Damascene /aː/ is LESS fronted than Coastal Syrian, so no strong imāla rule is declared here (contrast ar-LB). TOKENIZATION NOTE (word-initial diphthong digraphs): the inherited Levantine monophthongization AR_LEV_MONO_AY/AW rewrites the /aj//aw/ ATOMS that the ⟨َي⟩/⟨َو⟩ digraphs emit, but word-initially the onset digraphs يَ→/ja/ and وَ→/wa/ claim the fatha before an /aj//aw/ atom can form, so ⟨وَيْ⟩ came out *[waj] (وَين *wajn) and ⟨يَوْ⟩ *[jaw]. The two sequences are therefore spelled out in `graphemes` as وَيْ→[weː] (وَيْن → weːn 'where', Cowell 1964; the monophthong /eː/ from /aj/ per Almbark & Hellmuth 2015 §1.1) and يَوْ→[joː] (يَوْم → joːm 'day'), which is the same monophthongization reached from the spelling instead of from the atom — exactly the device the Hejazi/Qassimi specs use for their identical seam. The explicit sukun in both keys keeps حَيَوان /ħajawaːn/ and يَوَدّ /jawadd/ — where the glide carries its own vowel and there is no diphthong — out of the rule.

sources.author: Almbark, R. & Hellmuth, S.
sources.title: Acoustic analysis of the Syrian Arabic vowel system
sources.notes: READ in full. Damascus/Syrian vowel system: five long /iː eː aː oː uː/, three short contrastive /i a u/; mid short [e o] and schwa are allophones of /i u/ (§3-4). Mid-long /eː oː/ from /aj aw/ coalescence (§1.1). /aː/ higher/fronter in Coastal Syria than Damascus (p.2).

sources.author: Fadda, H.
sources.title: Language Variation in Western Amman
sources.publisher: MA thesis, Canada Institute of Linguistics (CanIL)
sources.notes: READ (phonology chapter). Ammani koine consonants = urban Palestinian [ʔ t d dˤ ʒ], 'identical to other Levantine cities' (pp.27-28). Qaf labels (Al-Wer & Herin 2011:60): [g] gilit, [q] qeltu, [ʔ] Jerusalem, [k] rural West Bank. Vowels: short /i u a/, long /iː uː eː oː aː/ (Al-Wer 2011:4). Diphthongs /aj aw/→[eː oː] except before glide: bajt→beːt, sˤawt→sˤoːtˤ (p.30).

sources.author: Cowell, M.W.
sources.title: A Reference Grammar of Syrian Arabic (Based on the Dialect of Damascus)
sources.notes: Standard Damascus grammar. Primary text NOT obtained (Internet Archive borrow-only, no full text); its Damascene vowel classification (5 long / 5 short + schwa) is cited here only as reported and tested by Almbark & Hellmuth 2015 §1.3.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic
sources.notes: Emphasis (pharyngealization) spreading onto neighbouring low vowels. Not obtained in full; cited for the widely-described emphasis-spreading process.

location.notes: Damascus — the city the Damascene variety is named for. [Wikidata Q3766 P625]

stress.notes: Arabic stress is QUANTITY-SENSITIVE: it falls on a syllable because that syllable is heavy, not because of any orthographic ending, so no ending table can express it. The cascade (Ryding, A Reference Grammar of MSA, CUP 2005, §2.3; Watson, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic, OUP 2002, ch.3): a superheavy final syllable (CVːC/CVCC) takes the stress (kiˈtaːb); otherwise a heavy penult (CVː/CVC) does (muˈdarris); otherwise the antepenult (ˈmadrasa). max_onset=1 because Arabic onsets are obligatory and hold exactly one consonant — a medial cluster splits, closing the preceding syllable, and that boundary is what makes the penult of mudarris heavy. INPUT CONTRACT: weight is only recoverable from a complete transcription, so this requires diacritized input; an undiacritized skeleton has no vowels to weigh. The registered wikipron gold carries no stress marks, but the benchmark strips stress from both sides by default, so this costs no PER.
